frameworks.name: emergent property inference (EPI)
frameworks.one_line: deep-network inverse problem solver targeting computational properties, not datasets

frameworks.name: probabilistic / Bayesian parameter inference
frameworks.one_line: modern probabilistic toolkit applied to mechanistic circuit models

frameworks.name: mechanistic circuit modeling
frameworks.one_line: system-of-equations neural circuit models whose parameters EPI infers

core_question: How can one identify circuit-model parameters consistent with hypothesized emergent computational properties?
key_assumptions: circuit models are characterized by emergent computational properties rather than fits to a particular dataset
parameter distributions yielding such properties can be learned by deep neural networks
manipulations: demonstrated EPI in stomatogastric ganglion as motivating example
applied EPI to models of primary visual cortex and superior colliculus
compared scaling in parameter dimension against alternative inverse-problem techniques
key_findings: EPI affords precise control over computational properties of inferred parameters
EPI scales better with parameter dimension than alternative inference techniques
novel parametric structure surfaced in V1 and superior colliculus circuit models
interpretations: deep-network inverse inference can interrogate theoretical circuit models at the level of emergent properties
examining inferred parameter structure yields new theoretical insights into neural computation
